app.title: ChironAI
app.subtitle: Local, model-agnostic RAG layer for developers
app.session.unavailable: Backend session is unavailable. Tabs will work once the server finishes starting — click Retry or wait a moment.
common.error.generic_hint: Check that the backend is running, then try again. See Logs for details.
common.error.network_hint: Could not reach the backend. Confirm the server is running and refresh.
common.error.boundary_hint: Reload the page or switch tabs. If it keeps happening, check Logs.
crawler.empty_sources: No crawl sources found. Run crawler first.
crawler.header.title: Crawler / Indexer
crawler.sources.title: Crawl Sources
crawler.modal.embed_hint: Same pool as in RAG / Qdrant. Choose a model for this indexing run, or leave Server default to use the saved RAG embedding model.
crawler.modal.parallel_workers_hint: Higher values can speed up indexing, but may increase provider timeouts on limited GPU/VRAM.
crawler.md_pipeline.title: MD Pipeline
crawler.md_pipeline.preview_hint: Run this pipeline on the selected file
crawler.md_pipeline.add_step_intro: Choose a step type. Each card shows a short description and an example.
crawler.modal.seed_urls_hint: Additional entry points for the crawler. Each URL should be on a new line or separate entry.
crawler.pipeline.delete_message: Are you sure you want to delete the pipeline "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
crawler.md_pipeline.preview.title: Preview result
crawler.md_pipeline.params.substring_pla: Substring (line containing this will be deleted)
rag.empty_collections: No collections found or Qdrant is not reachable.
providers.intro: Register OpenAI-compatible upstream APIs (base URL + API key) and review extension-backed providers used by LLM Proxy builds.
providers.empty_description: Add an OpenAI-compatible gateway to use it in the LLM Proxy build wizard.
providers.extensions_hint: These providers come from enabled extensions (for example ollama-provider). Manage them on the Extensions tab.
providers.extensions_empty_description: Enable an LLM provider extension and wait for runtime bootstrap to finish.
providers.form.api_key_required: API key is required when creating a provider
providers.form.api_key_unchanged: Leave blank to keep the current key
providers.form.manual_models: Manual model ids (one per line, optional)
settings.title: Settings
settings.language.title: Language
settings.language.hint: Choose the interface language for sidebar navigation and localized labels.
settings.theme.title: Theme
settings.general.title: General
settings.developer_mode.label: Developer mode
settings.developer_mode.hint: Enables the Developer Tools section in the sidebar (Testing, CoreUI Showcase, Dev Documentation, Swagger, Performance).
settings.tour.label: Product tour
settings.tour.hint: Replay the first-run walkthrough of Dashboard, Builds, Providers, Help, and Settings.
settings.db_path.label: Database path
settings.server_port.label: Server port
settings.server_port.hint_restart: Changing the server port takes effect after restarting the app.
settings.server_port.hint_env: SERVER_PORT is set, so this process uses {port}.
settings.server_port.hint_switch: Restart the app to switch from {active} to {next}.
settings.server_port.invalid: Server port must be between {min} and {max}
settings.log_poll.label: Log auto-update interval (ms)
settings.service_poll.label: Service status poll interval (seconds)
settings.service_poll.hint: How often the app refreshes extension, provider, and RAG/Qdrant status (sidebar dots and polling).
onboarding.language.title: Choose your language
onboarding.language.body: Pick English or Ukrainian for the interface. You can change this anytime in Settings.
onboarding.first_run.welcome.title: Welcome to ChironAI
onboarding.first_run.welcome.body: This short tour highlights the main areas: dashboard health, LLM Proxy builds, Providers, Crawler / Indexer, in-app Help, and Settings.
onboarding.first_run.dashboard.title: Dashboard
onboarding.first_run.dashboard.body: Check service health, GPU metrics, and quick links to start the stack or open logs.
onboarding.first_run.builds.title: LLM Proxy Builds
onboarding.first_run.builds.body: Create named builds that clients reference as OpenAI `model` ids — each with its own provider, RAG collection, and parameters.
onboarding.first_run.providers.title: Providers
onboarding.first_run.providers.body: Add custom OpenAI-compatible upstream APIs or review extension providers before picking one in the build wizard.
onboarding.first_run.crawler.title: Crawler / Indexer
onboarding.first_run.crawler.body: Crawl documentation sources, optionally clean markdown, and create Qdrant collections that power RAG retrieval in builds.
onboarding.first_run.help.title: Help knowledge base
onboarding.first_run.help.body: Browse operator guides for builds, RAG, providers, extensions, and troubleshooting. Field-level (i) buttons open the same articles in context.
onboarding.first_run.settings.title: Settings
onboarding.first_run.settings.body: Tune server port, theme, developer mode, and proxy defaults. You can restart this tour from Settings later.
onboarding.tour.builds.wizard.title: Create or edit a build
onboarding.tour.builds.wizard.body: This wizard defines a stable API model id plus provider, RAG collection, privacy, and generation parameters.
onboarding.tour.builds.id.title: Build id
onboarding.tour.builds.id.body: The id becomes the OpenAI `model` string clients send. Pick something short, unique, and lowercase.
onboarding.tour.builds.provider.title: Provider and model
onboarding.tour.builds.provider.body: Pick an upstream LLM provider and model. Add custom OpenAI-compatible gateways first in Providers if they are not listed yet.
onboarding.tour.builds.rag.title: Per-build RAG collection
onboarding.tour.builds.rag.body: Override the global Qdrant collection for this build only. Collections are created in Crawler / Indexer — leave empty to inherit the server default.
onboarding.tour.builds.save.title: Save the build
onboarding.tour.builds.save.body: When finished, save here. The build appears in GET /v1/models for external clients.
onboarding.tour.extensions.intro.title: Extensions
onboarding.tour.extensions.intro.body: Extensions add LLM providers, Docker services, and extra CoreUI tabs. Install from Registry or enable bundled ones.
onboarding.tour.extensions.views.title: Installed vs Registry
onboarding.tour.extensions.views.body: Use Installed to manage running extensions. Open Registry to browse available packages and install new capabilities.
onboarding.tour.prompts.new.title: Prompt templates
onboarding.tour.prompts.new.body: Templates become system prompts for builds. Create reusable instructions for coding, review, or support workflows.
onboarding.tour.prompts.editor.title: Structured editor
onboarding.tour.prompts.editor.body: Edit title, description, and body sections—or switch to raw mode. Use the in-editor assistant for linting and structure hints.
onboarding.tour.providers.intro.title: Upstream providers
onboarding.tour.providers.intro.body: Register OpenAI-compatible gateways (base URL + API key) or review extension-backed providers used by builds.
onboarding.tour.providers.custom.title: Custom providers
onboarding.tour.providers.custom.body: Create, test, and enable custom upstream APIs. Saved providers appear in the LLM Proxy build wizard provider dropdown.
onboarding.tour.providers.extensions.bod: Bundled extensions such as ollama-provider register here automatically. Open Extensions to install or manage them.
onboarding.tour.crawler.intro.title: Indexer / Crawler
onboarding.tour.crawler.intro.body: Two modes: crawl sources into markdown stores, then embed chunks into Qdrant collections for RAG builds.
onboarding.tour.crawler.section_tabs.bod: Switch between managing crawl sources and editing markdown cleanup pipelines applied before chunking.
onboarding.tour.crawler.sources_panel.bo: Each row shows ID, URL, last crawl time, and indexed page count. Select rows for batch actions.
onboarding.tour.crawler.add_source.title: Add Source
onboarding.tour.crawler.add_source.body: Register a URL, max depth, Playwright engine, optional seed URLs, and doc_only filter.
onboarding.tour.crawler.crawl_one.title: Crawl one source
onboarding.tour.crawler.crawl_one.body: Re-fetch the highlighted source and watch the indexed page count grow in the table.
onboarding.tour.crawler.crawl_batch.body: Use Crawl selected or Crawl ALL to refresh multiple sources in one run.
onboarding.tour.crawler.source_detail.bo: Expand a row to inspect seed URLs and recently crawled pages before indexing.
onboarding.tour.crawler.create_collectio: Pick sources, embedding model, and chunk parameters to upsert vectors into Qdrant.
onboarding.tour.crawler.collection_embed: Uses the same provider/model pool as the RAG tab. Tune parallel workers for your GPU/VRAM.
onboarding.tour.crawler.collection_chunk: Chunk max/min, confidence threshold, and top_k influence retrieval quality — start conservative, then iterate.
Optional markdown cleanup (strip boilerplate, reject low-signal bodies) before chunks are embedded.
Choose or create a named pipeline, save changes, and preview transforms on a sample file.
onboarding.tour.crawler.pipeline_steps.b: Stack step types such as strip meta blocks, delete boilerplate lines, or reject nav-heavy pages.
onboarding.tour.crawler.next_rag.title: Next: RAG and builds
onboarding.tour.crawler.next_rag.body: After indexing, verify the collection in the RAG tab, then attach it to an LLM Proxy build in the wizard.
trace.summary.token_lead_suffix: (serialized message size / 4), not provider billing usage.
trace.summary.token_mismatch: Sum of per-step model estimates (prompt {prompt}, completion {completion}) differs from trace totals — partial run or legacy trace.
trace.summary.no_rag_steps: No rag_query steps in this trace.
trace.summary.no_skills: No skills metadata or load_skill steps.
trace.summary.server_tools_hint: Order of tool_calls across all model turns (includes rag_query / load_skill when the model asked for them).
trace.summary.ide_passthrough_hint: Tool batch returned to the client for execution outside the proxy process.
